Rubus serpens is a species of bramble, a type of shrub or vine that is part of the Rubus genus. It is a type of thorny plant that produces edible fruits and flowers.

The Rubus genus, in general, has been associated with various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The fruits of Rubus species, such as raspberries and blackberries, are rich in vitamins, minerals, and phytochemicals, which may help to support cardiovascular health and reduce the risk of certain diseases.

The bioactive constituents of Rubus species include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and anthocyanins, which are responsible for their antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties.

The most common herbal preparations made with Rubus species include teas, infusions, and jams made from the fruits, as well as extracts and tinctures made from the leaves and stems.
